summary: Replaced the 206-line assembly implementation of ftwalljump.s with a new C translation in src/melee/ft/ftwalljump.c for func_8008169C, identified by comments as Interrupt_Walljump. The PR also updated build lists to compile the C object, added minimal Fighter, CollData, ftData, and ftCommonData fields needed by the translation, declared two external helper functions using UnclePunch map names as guidance, and renamed ftData::items to x48_items to preserve offset-based naming. The author explicitly noted that variable names were tentative and that fighter.h was intentionally reduced to used variables only.
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/ft/ftwalljump.s and added src/melee/ft/ftwalljump.c containing BOOL func_8008169C(HSD_GObj*), with comments linking it to UnclePunch Map file name Interrupt_Walljump.
Updated obj_files.mk so ftwalljump.o is built from src/melee/ft/ftwalljump.o instead of asm/melee/ft/ftwalljump.o.
Added ftwalljump.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.
Added prototypes in include/functions.h for func_800567C0, commented as Collision_GetPositionDifference, and func_800C1E64, commented as AS_203_PassiveWalljump_Walljump.

Expanded and reshaped src/melee/ft/fighter.h with only the fields needed by this C translation, including Vec2/Vec3 aliases, CollData, wall collision indices, environment flags, fighter position/delta fields, stick fields, walljump timers, wall side, walljump usage counter, and ftCommonData offsets x768, x76C, x770, and x774.
Changed ftPeach_OnLoad to use ftDataInfo->x48_items after ftData::items was renamed to x48_items.
smart_moves: Used offset-preserving struct fields and filler arrays instead of trying to fully model Fighter or ftData prematurely, matching the PR body statement that fighter.h structs only contain used variables.
Kept uncertain semantics visible through tentative x-offset names and comments rather than overclaiming exact game logic.
Cross-referenced UnclePunch map names in comments for both the walljump interrupt and helper calls, giving future decompilers searchable semantic anchors without renaming the symbols outright.
Introduced a MAX_WALLJUMP_INPUT_FRAMES macro for the repeated 254 sentinel value, making the C logic easier to read while still reflecting the assembly behavior.
Separated collision data into a CollData substruct at Fighter offset 0x6F0, which made repeated ECB, environment flag, and wall index accesses clearer in the decompiled walljump routine.
decomp_lessons: For early fighter decompilation, partial structs with explicit offsets and filler arrays can unblock matching without requiring the full Fighter layout to be known.
When a function's semantic names are not fully proven, preserve offset-based names such as x210C_walljumpInputTimer and x2110_walljumpWallSide and document uncertainty in comments.
External map names can be valuable evidence for provisional function comments, but this PR did not rename func_800567C0 or func_800C1E64 to those names.
Build-system swaps from asm to C require both removing the asm object from TEXT_O_FILES and adding the corresponding source object to the epilogue list when applicable.
Renaming a struct field to include its offset, such as items to x48_items, may require touching existing character code that used the old field name.
naming_conventions: Used x-offset field naming for uncertain or layout-driven fields, e.g. x48_items, x6F0_collData, x134_envFlags, x160_rightwall_index, x174_leftwall_index, x210C_walljumpInputTimer, and x2110_walljumpWallSide.
Used local variable suffixes reflecting original registers or stack slots, e.g. pCharData_r31, pCollData_r6, wallSideFlag_r0, wallSide_f31, wallPos_sp24, and ecb_sp18.
Used comments with UnclePunch map names rather than replacing unknown doldecomp symbols, e.g. Interrupt_Walljump, Collision_GetPositionDifference, and AS_203_PassiveWalljump_Walljump.
Used ftCommonData fields named only by offset, x768, x76C, x770, and x774, with a comment that the full size was still to be determined.
Renamed ftData::items to ftData::x48_items to align the field name with its known offset.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated the original stack temporaries into Vec locals wallPos_sp24 and ecb_sp18 plus an unused dummy array, preserving stack-shape clues from the assembly.
Modeled the signed wall side constants from sdata2 as -1.0f and 1.0f and the zero constant as 0.0f in C.
Used ternary absolute-value logic for deltaX_f1 to match the fcmp/fneg pattern from the assembly.
Kept exact threshold and timer comparisons against ftCommonData offsets x768, x76C, x770, and action parameter x774.
Preserved the walljump action call signature pattern func_800C1E64(gobj, 0xCB, common->x774, walljumpUsed, wallSide).
Matched the wall collision path by choosing left ECB data and x174_leftwall_index when the 0x800 environment flag is set, otherwise right ECB data and x160_rightwall_index.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body itself cautioned that the ftwalljump.c variable names were tentative and needed more time to work out the game logic.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename func_8008169C or confirm the final walljump input/timer field names.
Search for subsequent Fighter struct cleanup touching x210C_walljumpInputTimer, x2110_walljumpWallSide, or x1969_walljumpUsed.
Search for later declarations or renames of func_800567C0 and func_800C1E64 based on Collision_GetPositionDifference or AS_203_PassiveWalljump_Walljump.
Check whether ftCommonData offsets x768, x76C, x770, and x774 were later given semantic names.
Verify whether the partial CollData fields introduced here survived later collision header refactors.
